content: We can frame existing Self-Refine frameworks as a single-perspective primitive LLM self-reflection mechanism, and integrate multiple inconsistent solving perspectives from cutting-edge self-reflection research into Self-Refine’s iterative feedback loop to create a multi-perspective self-refinement framework that retains Self-Refine’s core advantage of requiring no extra training or supervised data. This hybrid framework would resolve the key limitation of vanilla Self-Refine that it can get stuck in local optima locked by the LLM’s inherent biased single worldview.
